_comment: AC1 (333.4) e2e fixture B — the SAME compound filter as fixture A (composition_sop_gui_export.json), (3-5) OR (entry:2 & exit:4) OR (3-5 & entry:2), but authored the way a human would write it directly in core's ComplexFilterParam: the repeated raypath [3,5] is DEDUPED to a single simple filter (id 1) and reused across clauses, so the composition is [[1],[2],[1,3]] over only 3 simple filters (vs fixture A's 4, no-dedup, [[1],[2],[3,4]]). Structurally different, semantically identical. Everything else (crystal, scene, render, ray budget) matches fixture A byte-for-byte so a render-compare isolates the filter encoding. High PSNR between A and B proves the GUI's ExpandSopToClauses expansion is the same gate as the idiomatic core expression.
